health.mental_health: Processing acute grief after the recent death of her younger sister; has persistent sadness, disrupted sleep, lower appetite on some days, and occasional difficulty concentrating at work. She is considering short-term grief counseling but feels private about it.
recent_visits: Ophthalmology follow-up at NYU Langone for glaucoma pressure check (last month)
Endocrinology/primary care visit for A1C review and medication adjustment (6 weeks ago)
DEXA scan at Mount Sinai for osteoporosis monitoring (3 months ago)
Podiatry visit for diabetic foot exam and nail care (2 months ago)
Telehealth visit with primary care about insomnia and grief (2 weeks ago)

finance.debt: Carries a small remaining balance on a home equity line used for apartment renovations and recent funeral-related costs; worries about ongoing co-pays and medication costs on a fixed income.
recent_transactions: Paid funeral home balance and memorial service catering deposit
Round-trip Amtrak tickets to Boston for family estate paperwork meeting
Pharmacy co-pays for diabetes and glaucoma medications
Monthly co-op maintenance payment and utilities
Donation to her sister's preferred charity in lieu of flowers

relationships: Widowed; spouse (Kenji Sato) died several years ago
Grieving the recent death of her younger sister, Mariko Tanaka, who was her closest confidante
Adult son, Daniel Sato, lives in Brooklyn and checks in frequently; their relationship is supportive but tense around estate decisions
Adult daughter, Erica Sato, lives in Philadelphia; Layla feels she is emotionally distant and avoids grief conversations
Longtime neighbor and friend, Rosa Martinez, who brings meals and walks with her in Riverside Park
A small circle from her Buddhist temple in Manhattan who provides condolences and practical help
social.social_media_activity: Mostly inactive; uses Facebook to view family updates and community notices, and uses WhatsApp for family group chats. She posted a brief memorial message but avoids sharing details of her grief publicly.
private_conversations: Discussing conflict with her brother-in-law about her sister's will and who should manage personal belongings
Admitting to her son that she sometimes feels guilty for not visiting her sister more often in the last year
Confiding in Rosa about sleep problems and occasional thoughts of 'not wanting to be here' (without a plan)
Talking with her temple mentor about whether she should retire sooner due to emotional exhaustion

schedule.work_hours: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–3:30 PM during the school year; limited summer hours for intake and planning.
regular_appointments: Ophthalmology check every 3–4 months for glaucoma monitoring
Primary care/diabetes follow-up every 3 months (A1C and blood pressure)
Weekly osteoporosis medication schedule and monthly pharmacy pickup
Physical therapy session once weekly for balance and fall-prevention exercises
Sunday morning service and community lunch at her Buddhist temple (when she feels up to it)
upcoming_events: School student support team meeting about a 9th-grade attendance intervention plan
Estate paperwork appointment with a family attorney in Midtown related to her sister's death
Follow-up eye pressure check and visual field test
Small family gathering on her sister's birthday at her son's apartment in Brooklyn
schedule.daily_routine: Wakes around 6:00 AM, checks blood glucose, takes morning meds and eye drops, rides the subway to school, keeps a structured counseling schedule with brief breaks, and tries to walk 20–30 minutes after dinner. Evenings are quieter since her sister's death; she often rereads old messages and sometimes falls asleep with the TV on.

professional.company: New York City Department of Education (public high school in Manhattan)
projects: Senior graduation and postsecondary planning (college and vocational applications)
Crisis response coordination and safety planning for at-risk students
Attendance improvement initiative with deans and social workers
Family outreach for students in temporary housing
Staff training refresh on mandated reporting and trauma-informed practices
professional.performance_issues: Since her sister's death, she has had a few moments of tearfulness between sessions and has requested lighter caseload days. She worries about compassion fatigue and has taken two bereavement days plus one additional sick day for insomnia; she is concerned colleagues may question her readiness to continue full-time.
workplace_relationships: Works closely with Assistant Principal Nadia Collins, who is supportive but expects clear documentation
Regular coordination with school social worker Marcus O'Neill on crisis cases
Occasional tension with a dean who pushes for quick disciplinary outcomes over counseling interventions
Trusted rapport with the school secretary who quietly helps her manage schedule changes

hobbies: Walking in Riverside Park and along the Hudson Greenway
Attending matinee performances when discounted tickets are available
Calligraphy and journaling (letters she never sends)
Cooking simple Japanese and Mediterranean meals adapted for diabetes
habits: Checks blood glucose most mornings and logs it in a notebook
Uses eye drops on a strict schedule and sets phone alarms
Keeps a fall-prevention routine at home (night lights, supportive shoes)
Has a nightly ritual of listening to voicemail messages from her sister, which can intensify grief
Occasionally drinks a small glass of whiskey to help her sleep, though she feels conflicted about it
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a co-op apartment on the Upper West Side; her sister used to visit often, and the quiet now feels heavy. She has started keeping the living room light on at night and is considering a part-time home aide after a recent near-fall.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Diabetes-conscious: limits sugary snacks, prefers high-fiber meals, smaller portions of rice/noodles, and keeps low-sodium options for blood pressure. When grief is intense, she sometimes skips breakfast and relies on tea and toast.
